items.title: Louisiana Legislature opens 2026 regular session with focus on education funding
items.summary: Lawmakers convened Monday in Baton Rouge for the start of the regular legislative session, with education funding and teacher pay topping the agenda for the coming weeks.
items.author: Staff Report

items.title: East Baton Rouge voter registration hits record high ahead of May primary
items.summary: New voter registration figures show a surge in East Baton Rouge Parish, with over 285,000 registered voters heading into the May 2026 party primary election.

items.title: U.S. Senate race heats up as candidates qualify for May primary
items.summary: Seven candidates have officially qualified for the Louisiana U.S. Senate race, setting up competitive Republican and Democratic primaries in May.
items.author: Political Desk

items.title: Governor signs infrastructure package with $1.2B for Louisiana roads
items.summary: The governor signed a bipartisan infrastructure bill that dedicates $1.2 billion to road repairs and bridge upgrades across the state over the next five years.

items.title: Metro Council approves Baton Rouge transit expansion plan
items.summary: The Metro Council voted 8-4 to approve a transit expansion plan that will add three new bus routes connecting underserved neighborhoods to employment centers.

items.title: Early voting locations announced for May 2026 party primary
items.summary: The East Baton Rouge Clerk of Court released the list of early voting sites for the upcoming May 16 closed party primary, with five locations across the parish.

items.title: Louisiana congressional delegation pushes for federal disaster relief funds
items.summary: Members of the Louisiana congressional delegation made a bipartisan push for additional FEMA funding to address ongoing recovery from recent severe weather events.
items.author: Capital Bureau

items.title: Candidate forum highlights differences on criminal justice reform
items.summary: A candidate forum hosted by the League of Women Voters featured sharp exchanges between U.S. House candidates over policing policy and sentencing reform.

items.title: State budget surplus sparks debate over tax cuts vs. education investment
items.summary: With a projected $800 million surplus, Louisiana lawmakers are split between using the windfall for tax relief or increasing spending on public schools and higher education.

items.title: Mayor-President Broome announces re-election bid for 2026
items.summary: Baton Rouge Mayor-President Sharon Weston Broome formally announced her campaign for re-election, citing progress on infrastructure and public safety during her tenure.
